key_points: Natural vegetation refers to plant life that grows naturally without human assistance
Virgin vegetation is natural vegetation that has remained undisturbed by humans
Flora represents plant species and fauna represents animal species found in a region
India is one of the twelve mega biodiversity countries of the world
Tropical evergreen forests grow in regions with more than 200 cm rainfall and dense vegetation
Tropical deciduous forests are called monsoon forests because they depend on monsoon rainfall
Thorn forests develop in regions with rainfall less than 70 cm
Montane forests occur in mountainous areas and vegetation varies with altitude
Mangrove forests grow in coastal tidal regions and have special root systems
Wildlife distribution depends on climate, vegetation and habitat conditions
examples: Example of evergreen forest location: Western Ghats and Andaman Islands
Example of deciduous forest tree: Sal
Example of thorn forest tree: Babool
Example of montane forest tree: Pine
Example of mangrove forest tree: Sundari
Example of migratory bird visiting India: Siberian Crane
formulas: Natural vegetation = Plants growing naturally in a region
Biodiversity = Variety of plant and animal species
Mangrove ecosystem = Coastal vegetation adapted to tidal water
Forest conservation = Protection of forests and wildlife
exam_tips: Understand the climatic conditions required for different forest types
Remember examples of trees and animals associated with each forest type
Study the role of forests in maintaining ecological balance
Focus on conservation measures such as national parks and wildlife sanctuaries
Remember important conservation projects like Project Tiger and Project Elephant
